title: Gilletiodendron pierreanum
taxonomy: Gilletiodendron pierreanum is a species of plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae. This flowering plant is native to the tropical regions of the Congo Basin. It is a member of the genus Gilletiodendron, which is a group of legume trees. The species is classified as a tree, with a broad and flat crown. Gilletiodendron pierreanum is also known by its other name, Piptadenia pierreana, which is sometimes used to refer to it.

The plant is traditionally used in local medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. intro: Gilletiodendron pierreanum, also known as the African white ironwood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gilletiodendron pierreanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used to make a tea to treat various health conditions.
